_README: THIS FILE IS AN EXAMPLE. IT IS NOT LOADED. IT IS NAMED mcp.example.json ON PURPOSE.
Rename it to .mcp.json ONLY if you know you want these, and only after editing every path
below to point at software that exists on YOUR machine. Shipping it live would break on
WHAT THIS FILE CAN AND CANNOT DO — the honest version:
CAN: declare how to launch a local MCP server that is ALREADY INSTALLED on your machine.
CANNOT: install that server. Cannot ship another vendor's desktop app. Cannot ship a data
corpus. Cannot install a Desktop Extension (.mcpb) — that is a SEPARATE CHANNEL,
enforced: admin controls can disable .mcpb installs while leaving plugins untouched.
SO: the iMessage reader is NOT bundled here, and it CANNOT BE. Neither can Apple Notes.
Both are Desktop Extensions. Install them at Settings > Extensions > Browse extensions.
iMessage additionally needs Full Disk Access, which only you can grant.
THE COST NOBODY TELLS YOU: every local server here PINS YOUR BRIEF TO A SLEEPING LAPTOP.
A scheduled task that needs local files or apps ONLY RUNS LOCALLY, and a local task is
SKIPPED when the machine is asleep. You trade the 7am brief for the corroboration.
Connect nothing here unless you can name the blind spot it closes and accept that trade.
NEVER PUT A SECRET IN THIS FILE. Anyone on the machine can read it. Use ${ENV_VAR}.

_lifelog._what: A wearable that records your conversations. Fills an ~~evidence[] slot.
_lifelog._honest: There is no drop-in replacement and the blind spot is narrow. Most people should not add this. It also records everyone around you, most of whom did not consent.
_lifelog._install: Vendor's own installer, then its CLI. This plugin cannot install it.
_lifelog._why_disabled: The paths below are examples. A venv path is not a library — it is one person's data corpus.

_notes_local_server._what: Some notes apps (e.g. Reflect) run a local HTTP server; the connector talks to that. Fills an ~~intent[] slot.
_notes_local_server._honest: The dependency is THE APP RUNNING ON YOUR MACHINE. A plugin cannot ship another vendor's desktop app. If the app is closed, this is dead.
_notes_local_server._substitute: Notion (hosted, no local server), Apple Notes (extension), a folder of Markdown, or mail-to-yourself — which needs no connector at all because the mail role already covers it.

_python_bridge._what: Shape only — a local python MCP server run from a virtualenv.
_python_bridge._honest: Anthropic recommends against bundling Python: compiled dependencies do not port. Only Node ships with Claude Desktop.

a_shell_bridge: An AppleScript/osascript extension — sometimes called 'Control your Mac' — is NOT listed here,
and setup will not recommend one. This is a decision, not an oversight.
It is not a connector. It is a SHELL. Its blast radius is your whole user account: files,
credentials, browser data, ssh keys. Desktop extensions run fully unsandboxed with full
And it composes badly with THIS system specifically. A zero-click remote-code-execution
affecting 50 extensions — triggered by a single malicious CALENDAR INVITE — was declined by
the vendor as 'outside our current threat model', on the stated position that locally
installed extensions are trusted. THIS ENGINE READS CALENDAR INVITES.
If you already run one, that is your call and your risk. This package will not talk you into it.
hosted_connectors: Gmail, Google Calendar, Todoist, Notion, Slack, Linear and the rest are NOT declared here.
They are not local servers — you add them once in Claude's own connector settings and they
work on every surface and every plan, including mobile, and they keep your brief
cloud-eligible. That is strictly better than anything this file can do.
Setup walks you through them. See references/VOLATILE.md for the current click paths.
